# Out-of-tree build for the QEMU TCG plugin libhybrid_handoff.so. Standalone
# from the vsim CMake build because the plugin links against host QEMU's
# headers + glib, neither of which exist inside the vsim build container.
#
# Usage:
#   make QEMU_ROOT=/local/nick/qemu_v5
#
# Output: libhybrid_handoff.so in this directory.

QEMU_ROOT ?= /local/nick/qemu_v5
CC        ?= gcc

GLIB_CFLAGS := $(shell pkg-config --cflags glib-2.0)
GLIB_LIBS   := $(shell pkg-config --libs   glib-2.0)

CFLAGS  := -O2 -fPIC -Wall -Wextra -std=c11
HYBRID_INCLUDE ?= ../include
INCLUDES := -I$(HYBRID_INCLUDE) -I$(QEMU_ROOT)/include/qemu $(GLIB_CFLAGS)
LDFLAGS := -shared
LIBS    := $(GLIB_LIBS)

.PHONY: all clean

all: libhybrid_handoff.so

libhybrid_handoff.so: hybrid_handoff.c \
                     $(HYBRID_INCLUDE)/hybrid/state_abi.h \
                     $(HYBRID_INCLUDE)/hybrid/state_abi_check.h \
                     $(HYBRID_INCLUDE)/hybrid/insn_match.h
	@if [ ! -f $(QEMU_ROOT)/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h ]; then \
	    echo "error: qemu-plugin.h not under $(QEMU_ROOT)/include/qemu/"; \
	    echo "       set QEMU_ROOT=<path> on the make command line"; \
	    exit 1; \
	fi
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) $(INCLUDES) $(LDFLAGS) -o $@ $< $(LIBS)

clean:
	rm -f libhybrid_handoff.so

content.text:
No - keep it in `hybrid_qemu/contrib/plugins/`. The workspace `qemu_plugin/` exists for plugins that depend on workspace-level hybrid headers (`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`, etc.) and need to outlive the QEMU fork; `hotloops.c` is a generic profiler with no hybrid-ABI coupling and rides on upstream's `contrib/plugins/meson.build` scaffolding, which you'd lose by moving it.

The dividing line worth keeping: workspace `qemu_plugin/` = hybrid-specific, out-of-tree; `hybrid_qemu/contrib/plugins/` = generic, in-tree. Move it only if you plan to detach hotloops from the fork's lifecycle.

# hybrid_sim

Workspace for the Andes hybrid simulator: QEMU 9.x (functional, MHz) + vsim
(SystemC + Verilator, cycle-accurate) sharing memory and architectural state
across a magic-CSR boundary.

Two submodules:

- `hybrid_qemu/` - QEMU fork hosting the TCG plugin
- `hybrid_vsim/` - vsim sources, including `hybrid/qemu_plugin/` (plugin source)

## Quickstart

```sh
git clone --recursive https://gitea.andestech.com/nick/hybrid_sim
cd hybrid_sim
./build.sh           # qemu, vsim, plugin (in that order)
./build.sh test      # ctest -L hybrid-e2e
```

Subcommands: `qemu`, `vsim`, `plugin`, `test`, `clean`. Run `./build.sh -h`
for the full list.

## Host dependencies

- C/C++ toolchain (clang preferred), CMake >= 3.20, ninja
- Python 3
- glib-2.0 dev (for the QEMU plugin)
- pkg-config
- Verilator (auto-detected; set `VSIM_VERILATOR_EXECUTABLE` if needed)
- libraries QEMU itself needs (pixman, slirp, etc. - see `hybrid_qemu/README.rst`)

## Outputs

After `./build.sh`:

- `hybrid_qemu/build/qemu-system-riscv64`
- `hybrid_vsim/build/vsim`
- `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so`

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Driver: build qemu + vsim + plugin, then add build dirs to PATH.
# Source this script to affect your current shell's PATH:
#   source ./setup.sh                       # build all
#   source ./setup.sh clean [target]        # clean qemu|vsim|plugin|all (default: all)

if [ -n "${ZSH_VERSION:-}" ]; then
  _SETUP_SRC="${(%):-%x}"
elif [ -n "${BASH_VERSION:-}" ]; then
  _SETUP_SRC="${BASH_SOURCE[0]}"
else
  _SETUP_SRC="$0"
fi

# Wrap the body in a function so strict-mode options stay local and don't
# leak into the caller's interactive shell (which would break zsh prompts
# under nounset).
_hybrid_setup_main() {
  if [ -n "${ZSH_VERSION:-}" ]; then
    emulate -L sh
    setopt err_exit no_unset pipe_fail
  else
    local -
    set -euo pipefail
  fi

  local ROOT QEMU_BIN_DIR VSIM_BIN_DIR PLUGIN_DIR QEMU_SRC PLUGIN_SO
  ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$_SETUP_SRC")" && pwd)"
  QEMU_BIN_DIR="$ROOT/build/qemu"
  VSIM_BIN_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim/build"
  PLUGIN_DIR="$ROOT/qemu_plugin"
  QEMU_SRC="$ROOT/hybrid_qemu"
  PLUGIN_SO="$PLUGIN_DIR/libhybrid_handoff.so"

  step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m \033[1m%s\033[0m\n' "$*"; }
  ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
  err()  { printf '\033[1;31m[err]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

  clean_qemu()   { step "cleaning qemu build ($QEMU_BIN_DIR)";   rm -rf "$QEMU_BIN_DIR"; ok "qemu cleaned"; }
  clean_vsim()   { step "cleaning vsim build ($VSIM_BIN_DIR)";   rm -rf "$VSIM_BIN_DIR"; ok "vsim cleaned"; }
  clean_plugin() {
    step "cleaning plugin build ($PLUGIN_DIR)"
    if [ -f "$PLUGIN_DIR/Makefile" ]; then
      make -C "$PLUGIN_DIR" clean
    else
      rm -f "$PLUGIN_SO" "$PLUGIN_DIR"/*.o
    fi
    ok "plugin cleaned"
  }

  if [ "${1:-}" = "clean" ]; then
    case "${2:-all}" in
      qemu)   clean_qemu ;;
      vsim)   clean_vsim ;;
      plugin) clean_plugin ;;
      all)    clean_qemu; clean_vsim; clean_plugin ;;
      *)      err "usage: source $_SETUP_SRC clean [qemu|vsim|plugin|all]"; return 1 ;;
    esac
    return 0
  fi

  step "1/3 building qemu"
  "$ROOT/build_qemu.sh"
  ok "qemu -> $QEMU_BIN_DIR/qemu-system-riscv64"

  step "2/3 building vsim"
  "$ROOT/build_vsim.sh"
  ok "vsim -> $VSIM_BIN_DIR/vsim"

  step "3/3 building hybrid plugin"
  if [ ! -f "$PLUGIN_DIR/Makefile" ]; then
    err "missing $PLUGIN_DIR/Makefile"
    return 1
  fi
  if [ ! -f "$QEMU_SRC/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h" ]; then
    err "missing $QEMU_SRC/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h - hybrid_qemu submodule not initialized"
    return 1
  fi
  make -C "$PLUGIN_DIR" QEMU_ROOT="$QEMU_SRC"
  ok "plugin -> $PLUGIN_SO"

  step "PATH: prepending build dirs"
  case ":$PATH:" in
    *":$QEMU_BIN_DIR:"*) ;;
    *) export PATH="$QEMU_BIN_DIR:$PATH" ;;
  esac
  case ":$PATH:" in
    *":$VSIM_BIN_DIR:"*) ;;
    *) export PATH="$VSIM_BIN_DIR:$PATH" ;;
  esac
  ok "PATH updated (this shell only)"

  step "env: exporting VSIM_QEMU / VSIM_PLUGIN for e2e scripts"
  export VSIM_QEMU="$QEMU_BIN_DIR/qemu-system-riscv64"
  export VSIM_PLUGIN="$PLUGIN_SO"
  ok "VSIM_QEMU=$VSIM_QEMU"
  ok "VSIM_PLUGIN=$VSIM_PLUGIN"

  if [ -n "${BASH_VERSION:-}" ] && [ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]:-}" = "${0:-}" ]; then
    err "script was executed, not sourced - PATH change is local to this subshell"
    err "to update your shell, run: source $_SETUP_SRC"
  fi
}

_hybrid_setup_main "$@"
unset -f _hybrid_setup_main 2>/dev/null

  CACHE PATH "Path to QEMU source tree used to build the hybrid TCG plugin")

# The ABI test is always built so struct layout drift is caught even in
# non-hybrid builds. It has no SystemC/Verilator dependency.
vsim_add_test(test_state_abi tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_abi PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_abi)

# Shared-mem smoke: glues MmapBacking + SimpleMemory and verifies bytes are
# visible through an independent mmap of the same file.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_smoke tests/hybrid/shared_mem_smoke.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_smoke PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_smoke ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_smoke)

# Section 9: validates the narrowed QEMU-virt RAM region + mmap composition.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_offset tests/hybrid/shared_mem_offset.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_offset PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_offset ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_offset)

vsim_add_test(test_state_loader tests/hybrid/state_loader.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_loader PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_loader)

vsim_add_test(test_abstract_command tests/hybrid/abstract_command.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_abstract_command)

vsim_add_test(test_resume_driver tests/hybrid/resume_driver.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_resume_driver PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_resume_driver)

vsim_add_test(test_insn_match tests/hybrid/insn_match.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_insn_match PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_insn_match)

vsim_add_test(test_resume_from_qemu tests/hybrid/resume_from_qemu.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_resume_from_qemu PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_resume_from_qemu)

vsim_add_test(test_memory_map tests/hybrid/memory_map.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_memory_map)

vsim_add_test(test_csr_trigger tests/hybrid/csr_trigger.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_csr_trigger PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_csr_trigger)

vsim_add_test(test_state_drain tests/hybrid/state_drain.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_drain PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_drain)

vsim_add_test(test_gdbstub_client tests/hybrid/gdbstub_client.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_gdbstub_client)

vsim_add_test(test_handoff_controller tests/hybrid/handoff_controller.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_handoff_controller PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_handoff_controller)

if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID)
  if(NOT EXISTS "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h")
    message(WARNING
      "VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON but qemu-plugin.h not found under "
      "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/. Build the TCG plugin out-of-tree via "
      "hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/Makefile on a host that has QEMU installed.")
  endif()
  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")

  # Round-trip e2e tests. Each entry registers one CTest case using the
  # parameterized roundtrip_e2e.sh harness. The harness skips (return 77)
  # when QEMU/plugin/ELF artifacts are missing, so the same CTestTestfile
  # works whether ctest is invoked from the container's /work/build or
  # from the host's build/.
  set(VSIM_RT_FIXTURES
    "handoff_roundtrip:3"
    "rt_pc_jump:0"
    "rt_sign_bits:0"
    "rt_abi_aliases:0"
    "rt_long_roi:0"
    "rt_all_gprs:0"
    "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
    "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
    "rt_csr_survival:0"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()

  # Tier-2: separate driver because it allocates /dev/shm and uses
  # --shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file to share RAM across
  # QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2.
  add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_shared_mem
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh)
  set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_shared_mem PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
  )

  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer).
  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout)
    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()
endif()

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Build vsim inside the env-stage container image.
set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"

if [[ ! -f "$VSIM_DIR/Dockerfile" ]]; then
  echo "missing $VSIM_DIR/Dockerfile - run 'git submodule update --init --recursive'" >&2
  exit 1
fi

cd "$VSIM_DIR"

podman build --target env -t vsim-env:latest .
podman run --rm \
  -v "$PWD:/work" \
  -v "$ROOT/include:/include:ro" \
  -w /work localhost/vsim-env:latest ./build.sh

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# End-to-end roundtrip:
#   1. QEMU 1 + plugin: drains state at `csrwi 0x7C0, 0`.
#   2. vsim: resumes, runs body, drains at `csrwi 0x7C0, 1`, exits 200.
#   3. vsim's --qemu-binary path: spawns QEMU 2 with -gdb -S -semihosting,
#      restores GPRs+PC via gdbstub, continues.
#   4. QEMU 2 hits the RISC-V semihosting ebreak-triple, exits with the
#      semihosting exit_code (low 8 bits of a2 in the existing fixture).
#
# Fixture-parameterized:
#   FIXTURE     name of .elf under hybrid/test/  (default: handoff_roundtrip)
#   EXPECT_RC   expected vsim process exit code  (default: 3)
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN         path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU        path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN      path to libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_ELF         path to <fixture>.elf  (overrides FIXTURE if set)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-handoff_roundtrip}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-3}"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77   # CTest skip code when SKIP_RETURN_CODE is set
  fi
done

STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3] QEMU 1: drain entry state -> $STATE_QEMU"
"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3] vsim: resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout 60 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run the hybrid e2e tests directly, bypassing the VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID
# CTest gate. Drives hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/*.sh against pre-built
# artifacts in this workspace.
#
# Env overrides:
#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64 (default: build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64)
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so (default: qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so)
#   VSIM_BIN     sim_<cpu> (default: first sim_* under hybrid_vsim/build/)
#   FILTER       regex; only run tests whose name matches

set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"
HARNESS="$VSIM_DIR/tests/hybrid"
TEST_DIR="$VSIM_DIR/hybrid/test"

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-$ROOT/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$ROOT/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
if [[ -z "${VSIM_BIN:-}" ]]; then
  VSIM_BIN=$(ls "$VSIM_DIR"/build/sim_* 2>/dev/null | head -n1 || true)
fi

step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
warn() { printf '\033[1;33m[skip]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
fail() { printf '\033[1;31m[FAIL]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

require() {
  if [[ -z "$2" || ! -e "$2" ]]; then
    fail "$1 not found: $2"
    fail "build artifacts first: 'source ./setup.sh'"
    exit 1

Created `run_e2e.sh` at workspace root. It:

- Defaults artifact paths to where `setup.sh` puts them (`build/qemu/...`, `qemu_plugin/...`, `hybrid_vsim/build/sim_*`); each is overridable via env.
- Hard-fails if any artifact is missing (no silent rc=77 like CTest does).
- Builds the test ELFs via `make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test` if any are absent.
- Drives all 13 e2e cases that `HybridConfig.cmake` would have registered (9 roundtrip fixtures + `rt_shared_mem` + 3 negative drivers).
- `FILTER=<regex>` to scope to a subset, e.g. `FILTER=rt_pc_jump ./run_e2e.sh`.
- Returns non-zero with a list of failed cases.

Usage:
```sh
source ./setup.sh        # build qemu+vsim+plugin, export VSIM_QEMU/VSIM_PLUGIN
./run_e2e.sh             # all
FILTER=rt_pc ./run_e2e.sh   # subset
```

Caveat: the test-ELF Makefile hardcodes `TOOLCHAIN=/local/nick/SW_Release/...`; on hosts without that path, run with `TOOLCHAIN=<your-path> make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test` first or export it before invoking the script.
